title: invoice — retention windows
description: Invoices are the tenant's canonical financial-record entity. Retention is 7 years default (10 for Enterprise with invoice_extended_retention). Credit notes retain the same window in the primary DB but MIGRATE to the compliance archive for indefinite retention (regulator may audit years later). Invoice lines cascade with parents. PDFs move from S3 hot (7 years) to S3 Glacier cold (indefinite). TenantErased archives ALL records before the FK CASCADE — the tenant sees data gone, regulators still have visibility.
compliance_regimes_referenced: gaap + ifrs — sequential immutable numbering; immutable-after-finalize
vat directive 2006/112/EC Art. 244 — EU VAT invoices retention 10y in most member states
us national tax law (26 CFR §1.6001-1) — 7-year retention baseline
canada income tax act §230 — 6-year retention (we round up to 7 for uniformity)
gdpr Art. 6(1)(b) — contractual necessity; retention outlives consent revocation
gdpr Art. 17 §3 — legal-obligation carve-out for financial-record retention
pci-dss 10.7 — audit trail retention 1 year hot + 6 years archive

hot.description: Recent active + first year for aging queries, dispute resolution, tenant self-service

cold.description: Financial-audit retention. Enterprise with invoice_extended_retention: 3650 (10 years).

purge.trigger: > 7 years past created_at (10 for Enterprise) AND status IS terminal (paid / void / refunded / written_off / uncollectible)
purge.action: MIGRATE to compliance archive (S3 Glacier with regulator metadata) — DO NOT hard-delete the DB row (audit history depends on the FK RESTRICT chain surviving)
purge.notes: Invoices in 'draft' status are never purged by the retention job — a stuck draft is a data-hygiene issue, not a retention concern. A decade-old draft would indicate abandoned data + a clean-up task for ops.

purge.trigger: Cascaded via FK RESTRICT — cannot be purged independently of parent invoice
purge.action: N/A — lines are never independently retained-out
purge.notes: The RESTRICT FK ensures lines outlive purge attempts on the parent — the archive step must copy lines with the parent.

cold.description: Credit notes are the highest-integrity retention class in the module. Every issued/applied credit note represents a financial reversal — regulators may audit years later.

purge.trigger: TenantErased cascade (via FK CASCADE) ONLY — never scheduled/timed
purge.action: Hard delete only on tenant erasure (after compliance-archive copy)
purge.notes: Applied credit notes still live on — resolution is recorded via applied_at + applied_to_type + applied_to_id but the row does not become purgeable. Rationale: every credit note represents a financial reversal that regulators, finance teams, and tax auditors all care about.

hot.description: S3 Standard-tier storage for 7 years. Enterprise: 3650.

cold.description: S3 Glacier after hot retention. Retrieval requires 3-12h thaw.

purge.trigger: Never auto-purged from Glacier — only on TenantErased
purge.action: S3 Glacier lifecycle rule migrates from hot to cold at 7 years (10 for Enterprise)
purge.notes: PDFs are immutable — regenerating an invoice produces a new PDF with a new S3 key; the historical PDF survives at the original path for audit reference.

hot.description: 10 years hot for all tenants — credit notes are higher-tier than invoices.

purge.notes: Credit note PDFs are retained indefinitely — same reasoning as CreditNote rows.

purge.action: Cascaded (snapshots are sub-fields, not separate tables)
purge.notes: The snapshots outlive individual customer profile changes — the invoice carries its historical billing details for its lifetime + compliance archive.

special_cases.action: ArchiveInvoiceFinancialRecordsOnTenantErasure runs FIRST (compliance-archive copy). THEN PurgeInvoiceDataForErasedTenant hard-deletes via FK CASCADE.
special_cases.notes: The compliance archive is regulator-facing, separate-encryption-keys, limited-access. Even after tenant erasure, regulators can audit the tenant's invoice history via their platform admin escalation path. Audit rows written during those events survive per audit module's outlives-source design.

special_cases.action: RedactCustomerFromInvoicesOnUserErased nulls customer_id + PII fields (billing_address.name / .phone / .email, customer_tax_id) on affected invoices + credit_notes.
special_cases.notes: The invoice/credit_note rows survive (financial retention per Art. 17 §3). Regulators can still audit by tenant_id + invoice_number; personal identifiers are redacted.

special_cases.action: Retention windows extended from 7 → 10 years across invoices + credit notes + PDFs.
special_cases.notes: Some regimes (EU VAT, Canadian federal tax, UK VAT with adjustments) mandate 10-year retention. Enterprise tenants operating in those jurisdictions activate this entitlement.

special_cases.case: Migration from S3 hot to Glacier
special_cases.action: S3 lifecycle rule tagged `invoice_hot_years=7` (or 10) triggers automatic migration.
special_cases.notes: Signed-URL generation transparently handles Glacier retrieval — the first access to a Glacier-migrated PDF queues a thaw job; subsequent accesses (within 3-12h) return the thawed URL. Enterprise tenants may configure keep-hot forever via config('invoice.retention.pdf_hot_years').

special_cases.action: Not purged, but ops-visible via health probe `invoice-stale-drafts`. Retention job ignores draft status.
special_cases.notes: A 90-day-old draft indicates abandoned workflow — data-hygiene issue, not retention. Ops decides whether to void + hard-delete or preserve.

cross_reference: The audit rows written by Auditable outlive the source rows per audit::retention.json — audit retention is 7 years regardless of source-row retention.

TenantErased cascades hard-delete via FK CASCADE, but the audit rows written during those events survive the cascade (per audit module's outlives-source design) + the compliance archive holds the source data.
The compliance/data-subject-rights module orchestrates UserErased across all modules including this one — the module registers `RedactCustomerFromInvoicesOnUserErased` as a compliance callback.
